1,750 views • Jan 15, 2026 AI pioneer Stuart Russell (UC Berkeley) and Kenneth Cukier of “The Economist” discuss the existential risk of Artificial Intelligence and the need to align AI’s goals with those of humanity. The media are agog with claims that recent advances in AI put artificial general intelligence (AGI) within reach. Is this true? If so, is that a good thing? Alan Turing predicted that AGI would result in the machines taking control. I will argue that Turing was right to express concern but wrong to think that doom is inevitable. Instead, we need to develop a new kind of AI that is … ...more
